Examples: external behaviors of enlightened — calm speech, steady posture, graceful movement. Inner state reflects outer — look for equanimity in action. Krishna describes next — model for aspirants.
shlokas.genz: Arjuna's like 'what's the vibe of someone locked in transcendence? How do they talk, chill, move?' Examples: signs of enlightened glow-up — calm talk, steady sit, graceful walk. Inner peace shows outside. Krishna's answer incoming — blueprint for us.
shlokas.genalpha: Arjuna asks: 'How does a person with steady smart thinking in quiet happiness look? How do they talk, sit, and walk?' It's like asking 'how can I tell if someone is really calm and wise inside by how they act outside?'

shlokas.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान् |
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān
ātmany evātmanā tuṣṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadocyate
shlokas.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O Pārtha, when a man gives up all varieties of desire for sense gratification, which arise from mental concoction, and when his mind, thus purified, finds satisfaction in the self alone, then he is said to be in pure transcendental consciousness.
shlokas.millennial: First sign: renounces all mental desires for pleasure — satisfied in self alone. Pure consciousness. Examples: drop chasing external validation/highs (social media, relationships, achievements) — find inner contentment. Self-sufficient happiness — no dependency. Practice: mindfulness/meditation for inner joy — transcend craving.
shlokas.genz: Krishna's first sign: drops all mind-made cravings for pleasure — content in self only. Pure vibe. Examples: quit dopamine chase (scrolls, likes, highs) — inner satisfaction maxed. Self-reliant happiness — no external dependency.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: when someone lets go of all wanting fun things that the mind makes up — and feels happy just inside themselves — that's a steady wise person. Like not needing more toys or candy to feel good — happy with your own heart.

shlokas.sanskrit:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः |
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ
vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir ucyate
shlokas.translation: One who is not disturbed in mind even amidst the threefold miseries or elated when there is happiness, and who is free from attachment, fear and anger, is called a sage of steady mind.
shlokas.millennial: Sign: undisturbed in suffering, not elated in happiness — free from attachment/fear/anger. Steady sage. Examples: even-minded in layoffs/promotions, calm in crises/joys — no emotional rollercoaster. Detach from outcomes — inner stability. Practice equanimity — mindfulness in ups/downs.
shlokas.genz: Steady mind: no freakout in pain, no hype in happy — free from attachment/fear/rage. Sage level. Examples: chill in Ls, no ego in Ws — balanced vibe. Detach from results — inner peace maxed.
shlokas.genalpha: Not upset in sad times, not too excited in happy times — free from sticking to things, being scared, or angry. That's a calm wise person. Like staying the same smiley you in rain or sun — no big ups or downs.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् |
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śubhāśubham
nābhinandati na dveṣṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā
shlokas.translation: In the material world, one who is unaffected by whatever good or evil he may obtain, neither praising it nor despising it, is firmly fixed in perfect knowledge.
shlokas.millennial: Unaffected by good/evil obtained — no praise/disdain. Fixed knowledge. Examples: receive bonus/criticism — neutral response. No emotional swing — steady wisdom. Practice non-judgment — accept without reaction. Inner fixity — true knowledge.
shlokas.genz: No attachment everywhere — good/bad comes, no hype or hate. Locked-in knowledge. Examples: get W or L — neutral vibe. No emotional tilt — steady wisdom. Non-judgment practice.
shlokas.genalpha: Doesn't get too attached anywhere — when good or bad things come, doesn't cheer too much or hate. That's strong smart thinking. Like getting a gift or losing a toy — stay calm and same inside.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः |
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yadā saṁharate cāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ
indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā
shlokas.translation: One who is able to withdraw his senses from sense objects, as the tortoise draws its limbs within the shell, is firmly fixed in perfect consciousness.
shlokas.millennial: Withdraw senses from objects like tortoise into shell — fixed consciousness. Examples: sense control — avoid distractions (social media, temptations). Inner withdrawal — focus on self. Meditation practice — retract senses for clarity. Self-mastery — key to wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Pull senses back from stuff like tortoise into shell — locked-in consciousness. Examples: detox from scrolls/temptations — inner focus. Meditation hack — retract for clarity. Sense control = power.
shlokas.genalpha: When someone pulls their senses away from fun things like a turtle pulling legs into shell — that's strong smart thinking. Like closing your eyes and ears to noisy toys — staying calm inside your safe shell.

shlokas.sanskrit:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः |
रसवरजं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
viṣayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ
rasa-varjaṁ raso ’py asya paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate
shlokas.translation: The embodied soul may be restricted from sense enjoyment, though the taste for sense objects remains. But, ceasing such engagements by experiencing a higher taste, he is fixed in consciousness.
shlokas.millennial: Forced abstinence — cravings remain; but higher taste (divine) ends them. Fixed consciousness. Examples: diet/quitting habit — willpower fails, but finding joy in healthier/spiritual alternatives sustains. Experience divine bliss — material tastes fade. Inner fulfillment > external.
shlokas.genz: Force-stop sense pleasures — cravings linger; but higher taste (source) kills them. Locked consciousness. Examples: quit scrolling — FOMO stays, but divine vibe/meditation joy makes it irrelevant. Inner high > external highs.
shlokas.genalpha: If you stop eating yummy things by force — you still want them; but when you taste something even yummier (like God's love) — you don't want the old ones anymore. That's steady happy thinking. Like stopping candy because you found a magic fruit that's sweeter and better forever.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः |
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaścitaḥ
indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ
shlokas.translation: The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them.
shlokas.millennial: Even wise trying to control senses — senses overpower mind. Examples: disciplined professional — distractions (emails, social) hijack focus. Senses impetuous — need strong practice. Caution: vigilance required — no complacency in self-control.
shlokas.genz: Even smart ones trying hard — senses overpower and hijack mind. Examples: focused grind — notifications/drama pull you away. Senses wild — need strong control game. Warning: stay vigilant — no autopilot.
shlokas.genalpha: Even a very smart person trying hard to control eyes/ears/etc. — the senses are so strong they grab the mind away. Like trying to sit still but your eyes see a toy and pull your thoughts away — need to be extra strong inside.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः |
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaścitaḥ
indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ
shlokas.translation: The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna warns: even a wise, disciplined person trying to control senses — senses overpower and drag the mind away. This highlights the challenge of self-control in a distracting world. Examples: you're a focused entrepreneur or manager, setting boundaries like no emails after 7 PM to prioritize family or self-care — but a late-night notification pulls you in, and suddenly you're scrolling for hours, losing sleep and balance. The verse is a reality check: senses are powerful adversaries; constant vigilance and higher practices (like meditation or devotion) are needed to master them. It's not about perfection but persistent effort — don't beat yourself up for slips, just get back on track. In your 30s, this resonates with work-life balance struggles, where digital distractions hijack productivity and peace.
shlokas.genz: Krishna's warning: even smart, trying-hard people get their mind jacked by senses — they're wild and forceful. Examples: you're grinding on a study session or content creation, but TikTok notifications pop — next thing, you're doom-scrolling for hours, focus gone. Verse hits: senses are OP bosses; need constant check and higher hacks (meditation, devotion) to win. Don't rage-quit after slips — respawn and grind. Relates to ADHD vibes or social media addiction — senses drag you, but awareness pulls back.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: even a very smart person trying really hard to control their eyes/ears/tongue — the senses are so strong they grab the mind away suddenly. Examples: like when you're trying to sit quietly and do homework, but your eyes see a toy or ears hear a fun sound — and poof, your mind runs off to play instead. It's like senses are naughty puppies pulling your thoughts — you need to be gentle but strong to bring them back. Don't feel bad if it happens — just keep trying with love.

shlokas.sanskrit:
ध्यायतो विषयान् पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते |
सङ्गात् सञ्जायते कामः कामात् क्रोधोऽभिजायते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate
saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate
shlokas.translation: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains the downward spiral: thinking of sense objects → attachment → desire → anger (when unfulfilled). This chain reaction is the root of many personal/professional pitfalls. Examples: you browse LinkedIn, see a colleague's promotion — attachment to success forms, desire for your own arises, then anger/jealousy if not achieved, leading to resentment or rash decisions like quitting impulsively. The verse is a psychological map: break at the contemplation stage through mindfulness — redirect thoughts to higher purpose. In your 30s, this helps manage envy, FOMO, or material cravings that derail career/family balance. Prevention: cultivate detachment early.
shlokas.genz: Krishna breaks down the L chain: think about sense stuff (scrolls, vibes) → get attached → want it bad (craving) → get mad when you can't have it. Examples: doom-scrolling TikTok trends — attach to aesthetic, crave the look/bag, rage when you can't afford or it flops. Verse = mental health map: stop at the thinking stage with mindfulness — redirect to higher vibes. Avoids jealousy/FOMO spirals — key for social media gen.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says: when you think too much about fun things like toys or candy — you start liking them too much (attachment), then you really want them (desire), and if you don't get them you get angry. Examples: like seeing a new toy on TV — you think about it all day, want it so bad, then cry or get mad if you can't have it. Stop by thinking happy thoughts about sharing or playing with what you have — no angry feelings.

shlokas.sanskrit:
क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात् स्मृतिविभ्रमः |
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात् प्रणश्यति ||
shlokas.transliteration:
krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ
smṛti-bhraṁśād buddhi-nāśo buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati
shlokas.translation: From anger, complete delusion arises, and from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.
shlokas.millennial: The spiral continues: anger → delusion → memory loss → intelligence destroyed → total downfall. This is a step-by-step breakdown of how unchecked emotions lead to ruin. Examples: in a heated work argument (anger), you forget company values (memory loss), make poor decisions (lost intelligence), like sending an impulsive email — leading to career downfall. The verse is a cautionary tale: interrupt the chain early with self-awareness/breathwork. In relationships or finances, rage decisions often cause irreversible damage — preserve intellect through calm.
shlokas.genz: Anger → full delusion → forget everything → brain fried → total L and back to square one. Examples: rage-reply in comments (anger), forget your values (delusion), say dumb stuff (memory loss), make bad choices (brain gone) — clout/reputation ruined. Verse = emotional damage map: stop at anger with chill techniques. Rage quits life — stay calm to win.
shlokas.genalpha: From anger comes big confusion, from confusion you forget things, from forgetting your smart thinking breaks, from broken thinking you fall down completely. Examples: like getting mad at a friend — you confuse everything, forget to be nice, think wrong, and end up losing the friend. Stop anger with deep breaths — keep your happy smart heart.

shlokas.sanskrit:
रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश् चरन् |
आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ||
shlokas.transliteration:
rāga-dveṣa-viyuktais tu viṣayān indriyaiś caran
ātma-vaśyair vidheyātmā prasādam adhigacchati
shlokas.translation: But a person free from all attachment and aversion and able to control his senses through regulative principles of freedom can obtain the complete mercy of the Lord.
shlokas.millennial: Free from like/dislike (rāga/dveṣa) — engage senses under self-control — attain divine grace/peace. Examples: balanced interaction with world — enjoy without attachment, avoid without aversion. Regulated freedom — disciplined yet joyful. Leads to inner clarity/mercy. In daily life: mindful consumption (food, media) — no extremes, peace follows.
shlokas.genz: No like/hate vibes — engage senses with self-control — get full divine grace/peace. Examples: balanced scrolling/eating — enjoy without addiction, skip without FOMO. Regulated freedom = calm. Mercy unlocks.
shlokas.genalpha: But someone free from too much liking or hating — plays with senses but keeps control — gets the big loving kindness and peace. Like eating candy without wanting all, or not getting mad at yucky veggies — stay calm and happy inside.

shlokas.sanskrit:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते |
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyate
prasanna-cetaso hy āśu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣṭhate
shlokas.translation: For one thus satisfied [in Kṛṣṇa consciousness], the threefold miseries of material existence exist no longer; in such satisfied consciousness, one's intelligence is soon well established.
shlokas.millennial: In divine grace/peace — all miseries end; mind clear, intelligence steady. Examples: devotion/meditation brings contentment — external pains (body, others, nature) lose power. Stable intellect — better decisions, less stress. Spiritual satisfaction — ultimate resilience.
shlokas.genz: In grace/peace — all pains gone; mind clear, brain steady fast. Examples: devotion vibe kills suffering — body/drama/nature Ls irrelevant. Stable smarts = better moves, no stress.
shlokas.genalpha: In happy kindness, all sad things go away; with clear heart, smart thinking stands strong quickly. Like when you feel loved — no more hurts, your thoughts become steady and happy fast.

shlokas.sanskrit:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना |
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
nāsti buddhir ayuktasya na cāyuktasya bhāvanā
na cābhāvayataḥ śāntir aśāntasya kutaḥ sukham
shlokas.translation: One who is not connected with the Supreme [in Kṛṣṇa consciousness] can have neither transcendental intelligence nor a steady mind, without which there is no possibility of peace. And how can there be any happiness without peace?
shlokas.millennial: Without union (yoga) — no intelligence/steady mind; no steadiness — no peace; no peace — no happiness. Examples: disconnected from higher purpose — scattered mind, anxiety, unhappiness. Connection (devotion/meditation) — foundation for peace/joy. Prioritize spiritual alignment — everything else follows.
shlokas.genz: No connection to higher — no smarts/steady mind; no steadiness — no peace; no peace — no happy. Examples: unplugged from source — scattered vibes, anxiety max, no joy. Plug in (devotion) — peace + happy unlocked.
shlokas.genalpha: Someone not close to the big friend — no smart steady thinking; no steady — no peace; no peace — no happy. Like not holding your parent's hand — you feel lost and scared; hold tight — feel safe and joyful.

shlokas.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते |
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ||
shlokas.transliteration:
indriyāṇāṁ hi caratāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyate
tad asya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi
shlokas.translation: As a strong wind sweeps away a boat on the water, even one of the roaming senses on which the mind focuses can carry away a man's intelligence.
shlokas.millennial: Wandering senses — mind follows — intelligence lost like wind-swept boat. Examples: single distraction (phone ping during focus work) — derails productivity. Senses control mind — chaos. Practice sense restraint — steady intelligence. Vigilance key.
shlokas.genz: Roaming senses — mind chases — smarts gone like wind blowing boat off course. Examples: one notification during grind — focus wrecked. Senses hijack — chaos. Restrain senses — steady brain.
shlokas.genalpha: When senses run around, mind runs after — smart thinking gets lost like wind pushing a boat away on water. Like eyes seeing candy — mind chases, forgets homework. Keep senses calm — thinking stays safe.

shlokas.sanskrit:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः |
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ||
shlokas.transliteration:
tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛhītāni sarvaśaḥ
indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā
shlokas.translation: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, one whose senses are restrained from their objects is certainly of steady intelligence.
shlokas.millennial: Senses fully restrained from objects — steady intelligence. Examples: digital detox, mindful eating — sense control = clear mind. Practice withdrawal — inner strength. Steady wisdom — foundation for success.
shlokas.genz: Senses locked from stuff — steady smarts. Examples: no-scroll zones, controlled vibes — clear head. Restrain = power up. Wisdom steady.
shlokas.genalpha: So someone who keeps senses away from fun things everywhere — has very steady smart thinking. Like not touching toys when it's homework time — your mind stays strong and calm.

shlokas.sanskrit:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी |
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti saṁyamī
yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyato muneḥ
shlokas.translation: What is night for all beings is the time of awakening for the self-controlled; and the time of awakening for all beings is night for the introspective sage.
shlokas.millennial: Ordinary people 'sleep' in spiritual darkness — self-controlled awake in light. Material 'waking' — night for sage. Examples: masses chase material (money, fame) — 'asleep' to truth; sage 'awake' to inner reality, sees material as illusion. Reverse perspective — prioritize spiritual over material. In life: while others hustle for externals, focus inward for true awakening.
shlokas.genz: What everyone sees as 'day' (material grind) is 'night' for the sage; what they see as 'night' (spiritual) is 'day' for the controlled. Examples: masses 'asleep' in clout chase — sage awake in inner vibes. Flip the script — spiritual > material.
shlokas.genalpha: What is night and sleep for everyone is awake time for the calm person; what is awake and play for everyone is night for the wise one. Like when kids play outside all day — the wise kid reads and thinks inside; when others sleep, wise one looks at stars and wonders.

shlokas.sanskrit:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् |
तद्वत् कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ||
shlokas.transliteration:
āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṁ
samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat
tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśanti sarve
sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī
shlokas.translation: A person who is not disturbed by the incessant flow of desires — that enter like rivers into the ocean, which is ever being filled but is always still — can alone achieve peace, and not the man who strives to satisfy such desires.
shlokas.millennial: Peace like ocean — desires flow in like rivers, but remains undisturbed/full. Desire-fulfiller never peaceful. Examples: endless wants (career, relationships) — chase one, more come. Be like ocean — accept without disturbance. Contentment — true peace. Practice gratitude — desires lose power.
shlokas.genz: Be like ocean — desires flow in like rivers, but stays chill/full. Desire-chaser never peaceful. Examples: endless wants (clout, bag) — satisfy one, more pop. Ocean vibe — accept without tilt. Gratitude hack — peace unlocked.
shlokas.genalpha: Be like the big calm ocean — rivers of wants flow in but it stays steady and full. Someone who tries to catch all wants never feels peaceful. Like too many candies make you sick — be happy with what you have, like the ocean always full and calm.

shlokas.sanskrit:
विहाय कामान् यः सर्वान् पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः |
निर्ममः निरहंकारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ||
shlokas.transliteration:
vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṁś carati niḥspṛhaḥ
nirmamaḥ nirahaṁkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigacchati
shlokas.translation: A person who has given up all desires for sense gratification, who lives free from desires, who has given up all sense of proprietorship and is devoid of false ego — he alone can attain real peace.
shlokas.millennial: Renounce desires, live desire-free, no ownership/ego — attain peace. Examples: minimalism, detach from possessions/achievements/identity — inner freedom. No 'mine' or 'I' — ego drop. Practice non-attachment — peace follows.
shlokas.genz: Drop all desires, live no-crave, no ownership/ego — real peace. Examples: minimalist vibe, detach from stuff/clout/self — freedom. No 'mine' or 'I did that' — ego gone. Non-attachment = peace hack.
shlokas.genalpha: Let go of all wants, live without wishing for things, no 'this is mine' or 'I'm the best' — get true peace. Like sharing all toys, not saying 'mine', not bragging — feel calm and happy inside.

shlokas.sanskrit:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति |
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ||
shlokas.transliteration:
eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyati
sthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛcchati
shlokas.translation: That is the way of the spiritual and godly life, after attaining which a man is not bewildered. If one is thus situated even at the hour of death, one can attain to the Brahman nirvāṇa.
shlokas.millennial: This brāhmī sthiti (divine state) — once attained, no delusion. Even at death — reach Brahman nirvāṇa (liberation). Chapter end: ultimate goal — steady divine consciousness. Examples: lifelong practice ensures peaceful death/liberation. No fear — eternal union. Live in this state — freedom now.
shlokas.genz: This divine state — once in, no confusion. Even at logout — hit Brahman nirvāṇa. Examples: endgame mode — steady consciousness = liberation. Practice now for peaceful exit. No fear — eternal W.
shlokas.genalpha: This is the godly calm way — once you have it, no more confusion. Even at the end of life — you reach the big peaceful forever light. Like living so calmly and lovingly — when it's time to go home, you go happily to the best place.
